# Mantle Bridge
> Mantle Bridge is the canonical smart-contract bridge for transferring supported assets between Ethereum and Mantle Network.

## What is Mantle Bridge
Mantle Bridge moves supported tokens between Ethereum Layer 1 and Mantle Network, an EVM-compatible Ethereum rollup. It uses canonical bridge contracts and mapped token representations rather than a liquidity pool or third-party relayer route. The bridge is self-custodial at the wallet level, although bridged assets may be escrowed in smart contracts. This independent dashboard provides information only and does not operate the bridge.

## How it works
1. Connect an EVM wallet, select Ethereum or Mantle as the source, choose a listed token, and enter an amount.
2. For an ERC-20 deposit, approve the bridge if required, then submit the Ethereum transaction.
3. The L1 bridge escrows or processes the source asset and sends a cross-domain message; the corresponding Mantle token is minted or released according to its mapping.
4. A withdrawal reverses that process, finalizes through Mantle’s rollup system, and requires an Ethereum claim transaction before the L1 asset is released.

## Key facts
- Ethereum mainnet is chain ID 1; Mantle mainnet is chain ID 5000.
- Mantle Sepolia testnet uses chain ID 5003.
- ETH pays Ethereum-side gas; MNT pays Mantle-side gas.
- Mantle is EVM-compatible and currently posts rollup data to Ethereum while validating state through OP Succinct ZK proofs.
- Supported ERC-20 pairs come from Mantle’s maintained token list; not every token is bridgeable.
- The L1 Standard Bridge proxy is `0x95fC37A27a2f68e3A647CDc081F0A89bb47c3012`.
- The L2 Standard Bridge is `0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010`.
- The official FAQ describes deposits as typically taking about two minutes and withdrawals as taking up to twelve hours.
- Bridge and rollup contracts are upgradeable, so users inherit smart-contract, governance, operator, and implementation risk.

## Common questions
### Is Mantle Bridge safe?
It is Mantle’s canonical bridge and its underlying network has published audits. Safety is not absolute: verify the official domain and consider contract upgrades, proof-system faults, operator outages, token-mapping errors, and phishing.

### Who controls my funds?
Your wallet retains its keys and signs every transaction. During bridging, assets or their backing may be held by bridge contracts until the corresponding withdrawal is finalized.

### What fees apply?
Deposits require Ethereum gas; withdrawals require MNT to initiate on Mantle and ETH to claim on Ethereum. Wallet approvals can add another Ethereum transaction.

### How long does bridging take?
The current official FAQ says deposits typically complete in about two minutes and withdrawals can take up to twelve hours. Older seven-day guidance predates the current bridge timing.

### Which assets and chains are supported?
The canonical route connects Ethereum and Mantle. Live assets are determined by the bridge token list; the official FAQ identifies ETH, USDC, USDT0, and legacy bridged USDT representations.

### How do I start?
Open the verified official bridge, connect an EVM wallet, confirm both networks and the token mapping, maintain the required gas assets, and consider a small test transfer first.

## App
- Dashboard: https://mantlebridge.github.io/
- App: https://mantlebridge.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Official bridge: https://app.mantle.xyz/bridge
Bridge FAQ: https://app.mantle.xyz/faq

Mantle documentation: https://docs.mantle.xyz/
Network quick access: https://docs.mantle.xyz/network/for-developers/quick-access
L1 contract addresses: https://docs.mantle.xyz/network/system-information/on-chain-system/key-l1-contract-address
L2 contract addresses: https://docs.mantle.xyz/network/system-information/off-chain-system/key-l2-contract-address
Token list: https://token-list.mantle.xyz/
Source code: https://github.com/mantlenetworkio
Audits: https://docs.mantle.xyz/network/more/audits
Official X: https://x.com/Mantle_Official
